description: Multi-phase plans currently in flight. Each phase references roadmap item titles; the activePhase section breaks the current phase into concrete tasks with status. Survives conversation compactions — a fresh session can pick up by reading this file.

phases.title: Plugin runtime — manifest, SDK, federation hooks
roadmapItems: Plugin runtime — manifest format, loader, SDK, federation hooks
phases.notes: Shipped. PluginManifest / definePlugin / server + client loaders / auto-mounted federation routes / hello-world reference plugin.

phases.title: Polls & Quizzes plugin — first SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Polls & Quizzes — first plugin (validates simple SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped end-to-end: schema migrations, REST routes, federation handler, real-time sync via babelr:ws custom event, translation wired manually (to be automated in Phase 3), list-detail view via the new ListDetailView primitive, sidebar-slot button opens the view.

phases.title: SDK gap-closure based on polls implementation
roadmapItems: Translation as default for plugin content — zero-config primitive
phases.notes: All four in-scope tasks shipped: component ergonomics (ComponentType + createElement), translation-by-default (<T>), WS helper (onWsMessage), and documented the server-safe/client-only file-split convention. Plugin authors now have one-liners for the friction points polls exposed. Phase 4 (project management) starts on a smoother SDK. Deferred items persisted to roadmap: 'Plugin SDK — stable re-exports of @babelr/client utilities' (P2, ships with Phase 5), 'Plugin DB abstraction — helper over drizzle for plugin tables' (P3, revisit after PM), 'Plugin test harness — standard pattern for plugin unit + integration tests' (P2, builds alongside PM), and cross-Tower voting is already covered by 'Validate polls federation across two-Tower staging' (P2).

phases.title: Project management plugin — complex SDK consumer
roadmapItems: Project management — second plugin (validates complex SDK case)
phases.notes: Shipped. 9 tasks: schema + CRUD, board list + kanban view, work item CRUD + sidebar preview, DnD reordering, comment threads (useChat), task + board embeds, real-time WS sync, AS2 federation (plugin inbox dispatch hook + outbound delivery + inbound handling), polish pass. SDK additions: <E> component, PluginInboxHandler, fastify.deliverToGroupFollowers. Roadmap item added for e2e federation testing on live Towers.

phases.title: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install, manifest freeze
roadmapItems: Plugin distribution — npm packaging, admin install flow, manifest freeze
Plugin SDK — stable re-exports of @babelr/client utilities
Plugin test harness — standard pattern for plugin unit + integration tests
phases.notes: Polls + PM prove the contract works end-to-end. Phase 5 freezes the manifest, ships npm packaging so third-party authors can build/distribute plugins, and batches the SDK re-export + test harness deferred items. Circle back after MVP gaps are closed.

phases.title: Market-MVP gaps — onboarding, PWA, email verification, 2FA
roadmapItems: First-run onboarding flow — guided server creation + language preference
PWA support — installable web app + offline shell as a quick mobile path
phases.notes: The 'someone tried to use this as a real product' cluster. Onboarding + PWA have the most user-facing impact; email verification and 2FA are table-stakes security before any public deployment.

activePhase.title: Market-MVP gaps — onboarding, PWA, email verification, 2FA
activePhase.goal: Close the gaps that would block a real user from adopting Babelr as their workspace. Onboarding flow makes first-run frictionless; PWA gives mobile users an installable app without native builds; email verification + 2FA are security table-stakes.
activePhase.sequencing: Onboarding first (highest UX impact, touches registration + server creation + language pref). PWA second (Vite PWA plugin, service worker, manifest.json — relatively mechanical). Email verification third (server-side, needs SMTP config). 2FA last (TOTP, recovery codes, most isolated).

tasks.title: First-run onboarding flow
tasks.resolution: OnboardingWizard component with 4-step stepper: profile (display name), language (auto-detects browser locale, 17 languages), server (create-or-join with discoverable servers list, skip option), done screen. Client-side detection: App.tsx shows wizard instead of ChatView when actor.displayName is null. Updates profile via PUT /auth/profile, creates/joins server via existing APIs. 25 i18n strings added to shared/strings.ts. All strings localized via useT().
tasks.details: Guided first-run experience for new users. After registration: (1) set display name + avatar, (2) set preferred language, (3) create or join a server, (4) create first channel. Stepper-style UI with skip-ahead. The registration form itself already exists — this wraps the post-registration steps into a cohesive flow. Server: detect 'needs onboarding' (no servers, no language set) and return a flag in the auth response. Client: OnboardingWizard component shown instead of ChatView when the flag is set. Must work for both self-hosted admins (create server) and invitees (join via invite link, skip server creation).

tasks.title: PWA support — installable web app + offline shell
tasks.resolution: vite-plugin-pwa with generateSW mode. manifest.webmanifest with Babelr branding, dark theme (#0a0a0a), standalone display, 192+512 icons (placeholder — replace with real artwork). Workbox precaches the app shell (14 entries), navigateFallback to index.html with denylist for /api, /ws, /uploads. Runtime caching: NetworkFirst for API with 5s timeout + 50-entry/5-min cache. iOS meta tags: apple-mobile-web-app-capable, black-translucent status bar, apple-touch-icon. autoUpdate register type so new versions install silently on next visit.
tasks.details: Vite PWA plugin (vite-plugin-pwa) with a service worker for offline shell + asset caching. manifest.json with Babelr branding, icons, theme color. Workbox runtime caching for API responses (network-first for messages, cache-first for static assets). Install prompt on supported browsers. iOS Safari meta tags for standalone mode. This is the quick mobile path — native apps (Capacitor) come later.

tasks.title: Email verification on registration
tasks.resolution: Schema: email_verified boolean + verification_token + verification_token_expires on actors. Server: register generates a UUID token with 24h TTL and sends via nodemailer (SMTP config from env: SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASS/FROM). GET /auth/verify?token= marks verified and redirects to app. POST /auth/resend-verification re-generates token with rate limiting implicit in the 24h TTL. SKIP_EMAIL_VERIFICATION=true env var bypasses for self-hosted. Client: VerificationBanner shows at top of ChatView when emailVerified is false, with resend button. ActorProfile now includes emailVerified. 4 i18n strings.
tasks.details: Server: email_verified boolean on actors table, verification_token + token_expires columns. POST /auth/register sends a verification email with a signed token link. GET /auth/verify?token=... marks the account verified. Unverified accounts can log in but see a banner + can't create servers or send messages (read-only). SMTP config via env vars (SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASS, SMTP_FROM). Resend endpoint with rate limiting. For self-hosted: optional SKIP_EMAIL_VERIFICATION=true env var.

tasks.title: Two-factor authentication (TOTP)
tasks.details: TOTP-based 2FA using otpauth. Server: totp_secret + totp_enabled on actors table. Setup flow: POST /auth/2fa/setup returns a QR code URI, POST /auth/2fa/verify confirms setup with a code. Login flow: if totp_enabled, the login response includes a 2fa_required flag; client shows a code input, POST /auth/2fa/challenge completes login. Recovery codes: 10 single-use codes generated at setup, stored hashed, shown once to the user. POST /auth/2fa/disable with a valid code turns it off. Client: 2FA section in SettingsPanel for setup/disable, code input modal during login.
